items.title: Johnson & Johnson CEO: 'We’re in the Golden Era of Medical Innovation'
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Johnson & Johnson Chairman and CEO Joaquin Duato believes that we are now in a “golden era” of innovation, particularly in the United States. Venture capital plays a vital early-stage role, but big pharma provides the infrastructure and market access. We visit Johnson & Johnson's innovation facility in Spring House to see how its early-stage innovation ecosystem serves patients and the local economy.
--------

items.title: Netflix Co-CEO Sees Media Industry 'Shakeout' Amid Warner Split
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Warner’s decision to split into two independent companies is a sign of a broader “shakeout” across a media industry that has become increasingly dominated by streaming and on-demand services, Netflix Inc. co-Chief Executive Officer Greg Peters said. “Everything is moving to streaming — everything is moving to on demand,” Peters said Thursday in an interview with Bloomberg Editor-in-Chief John Micklethwait at the Founders Forum Global conference. “There’s going to be a period of shakeout and transition associated with that.”
--------

items.title: How Trump’s ICE raids could affect the US labor market #politics #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>With Trump's ICE raids on the rise, reports are coming in that immigrants are staying away from their jobs. If the trend continues, the economic consequences in multiple sectors of the labor force could play out in a big way.
On this episode of the Everybody's Business podcast, we discuss what's going on behind the immigration crackdown https://t.co/SkK727QEvR</article>

items.title: Trump will still visit the UK, says Starmer
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"This will be a historic second visit for President Trump."

Prime Minister Keir Starmer says that Donald Trump will still be coming to the UK for a state visit after he gave the US president an invitation from King Charles when the trade deal was agreed in May http://bloom.bg/4l3DNht</article>

items.title: Britain's billionaire exodus shows cost of tax raid
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>The UK government says ending special tax privileges for the wealthy will bring in billions.
@BenStupples explains why a growing wealth exodus is putting their decision to the test https://t.co/Y2eY38GVD7</article>

items.title: #Wayve seeks to build fully autonomous #cars with #Nissan, #CEO says
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"We want to grow all the way to level five autonomy."
Self-driving software startup Wayve is seeking to make Nissan vehicles fully autonomous as part of its partnership, CEO Alex Kendall says.</article>

items.title: Gundlach says India is a long term investment opportunity
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>India is one of the "most bankable” long term investment opportunities, according to veteran bond manager Jeffrey Gundlach.
Read the DoubleLine founder’s take on America’s debt burden at the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum: https://t.co/fbtEkuAFHi</article>

items.title: Africa: Zimbabwe Gold Rally Brings Riches to Rural Artisanal Miners
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>The three-year surge in gold prices, driven to a record this year as US President Donald Trump’s trade war bolstered its allure as a safe-haven, is enriching Zimbabwe's more than 700,000 informal, or artisanal, miners. They are flocking to gold mining belts and throwing a lifeline to an economy that’s been in turmoil since the turn of the century. Bloomberg's Ray Ndlovu reports.
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>BYD Executive Vice President Stella Li discusses the outlook for sales in Europe, the impact of tariffs on the carmaker, and  competition in the electric vehicle industry. She talks with Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie on the sidelines of the Founders Forum Global conference in England.

items.title: What’s the Labubu doll got to do with China’s economy? #bloomberg
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>What do Pop Mart’s ever popular Labubu dolls have to do with China’s economy and the need for US-China trade talks? Rebecca Choong Wilkins explains.</article>

items.title: Middle East Escalation, Lower Gas Prices, Johnson & Johnson, Flying Cars | Wall Street Week
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>This week, Richard Haass of Centerview Partners tells us what the escalation of geopolitical tension between Israel and Iran could mean for oil prices. And President Trump’s call for $2 gas may please consumers, but in oil towns like Odessa, falling prices threaten jobs, investment and the economic foundation of the US energy heartland. Plus, an interview with Johnson & Johnson’s CEO on the golden age of medical innovation. Later, will flying cars soon be a reality?

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Middle East Escalation
00:11:29 - Odessa, Texas vs. Cheap gas
00:23:54 - Johnson & Johnson's Innovation Center
00:35:13 - Flying Cars
00:47:34 - Stanley Fischer's Legacy
--------

items.title: Middle East Tensions Escalate, Oil Prices Jump
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Centerview Partners Senior Counselor Richard Haass says that while geopolitical tensions cause fluctuations in prices, there would have to be ongoing conflict to see a sustained upward move in oil prices.
--------

items.title: Iran Fires Barrage of Missiles at Israel | Balance of Power Late Edition 6/13/2025
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Wendy Sherman, Former Deputy Secretary of State discusses the trading of missile strikes between Israel and Iran. She goes into what is to be expected in the coming days, Simone Ledeen,  Former Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for the Middle East discusses Israel's strikes on Iran and how Israel's defense is utilizing drone operations, Rep. Debbie Wasserman Schultz, (D) Florida says Israel has a right to defend itself after Iran launched retaliatory strikes at Tel Aviv.

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Bloomberg Balance of Power: Late Edition
00:01:29 - Michael Shepard, Bloomberg News
00:05:11 - Wendy Sherman, Former Deputy Secretary of State
00:13:13 - Simone Ledeen,  Former Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for the Middle East

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are John Bolton, Former US Ambassador, Rebecca Babin, CIBC Private Wealth Group, Stephen Trent, Citi, Bill Curtin, Hogan Lovells, Jess Menton, Bloomberg News, Jason Pride, Glenmede, Christopher Cain, Bloomberg Intelligence, Callie Cox, Ritzholtz Wealth Mgmt, Joe Cirincione, Center for International Policy, Andrew Kline, Park Lane, James Jeffrey, Former US Ambassador to Iraq & Turkey, Wendy Benjaminson, Bloomberg News.

items.title: President Trump’s executive order to make flying cars a reality #shorts #aviation #flying
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Will flying cars soon be a reality? Joby Aviation is working toward FAA certification for air-taxi services in cities, with plans to launch in 2026. Watch more on "Wall Street Week" here https://bloom.bg/3FYhWJi</article>
